name: Conn of the Hundred Battles
description: Conn Cétchathach, or Conn of the Hundred Battles, was a legendary High King of Ireland, said to have reigned in the 2nd century AD. He is a central figure in Irish mythology and pseudo-history, appearing in numerous tales and genealogies. His epithet derives from his supposed participation in and victory in numerous battles, solidifying his position as a powerful and influential ruler. Conn is considered an ancestor of several important Irish dynasties, including the Connachta, Ui Neill, and Ui Fiachrach, tracing their lineage back to him. His reign is often characterized by conflict and expansion, shaping the political landscape of early Ireland. The stories surrounding Conn often involve encounters with druids, prophecies, and otherworldly beings, highlighting the mystical aspects of early Irish kingship. The story of his reign provides valuable insights into the social structures, warfare, and spiritual beliefs of ancient Ireland. He is a symbol of strength, leadership, and the enduring power of lineage in Irish tradition. Conn's story is intertwined with the legendary figure of Bec mac Buain, whose presence caused Ireland to be barren until Conn banished him. Conn's legacy continues to be celebrated in Irish folklore and literature.
subtitle: High King of a Hundred Battles
achievements: Shaped the political landscape of early Ireland

keyMyths.title: The Prophecy of Conn's Reign
keyMyths.description: Druids prophesied Conn's reign, foretelling his many battles and the future of Ireland under his descendants. This prophecy established his legitimacy and the importance of his lineage.

keyMyths.title: Conn and Bec mac Buain
keyMyths.description: Bec mac Buain's presence caused barrenness in Ireland until Conn banished him, restoring fertility to the land. This tale highlights Conn's role in protecting the well-being of his kingdom.

keyMyths.title: The Battles of Conn
keyMyths.description: Conn's epithet comes from his participation in and winning of a hundred battles, solidifying his rule and expanding his territory. These battles are often recounted in Irish historical and mythological narratives.

sources.author: Geoffrey Keating
sources.work: Foras Feasa ar Éirinn (The History of Ireland)
sources.description: A 17th-century history of Ireland that includes accounts of Conn's reign.
sources.text: A 17th-century history of Ireland that includes accounts of Conn's reign.

sources.author: Annals of the Four Masters
sources.work: Annals of the Kingdom of Ireland
sources.description: A chronicle of Irish history compiled in the 17th century, containing references to Conn.
sources.text: A chronicle of Irish history compiled in the 17th century, containing references to Conn.
